title: Export to PDF as a whole document
excerpt: I see this has been raised a few times and feel it would be valuable to be able to export to a pdf, not only excel. To export a board or be able to select a line would benefit a lot of organisations from the feedback I h…

posts.author: LanieM (Lanie Muir)
posts.cookedHtml: I see this has been raised a few times and feel it would be valuable to be able to export to a pdf, not only excel. To export a board or be able to select a line would benefit a lot of organisations from the feedback I have read. We use a form which then lands on a board. If I need that board printed or a specific line, it takes valuable time in excel to change this how I need.

posts.author: johannesgeyer (Johannes Geyer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @LanieM ,

Welcome to the community.
You can use the app DocExport PDF Generator to print out your boards.

The app uses Word templates with placeholders. So you can specify which columns you want to export and create your own design. You can also use tables inside the Word template to reproduce the look of monday or create your own version.
https://www.docexport.com/help/template/colors/

It’s also possible to create a monday automation so that the report is automatically send via email every day / week / month.
In the DocExport Template Gallery you can find examples you can then modify to fit your needs.

DocExport works with formulas, mirror-columns, images, QR-Codes, Charts and Column Summaries:

800×562 30.9 KB

posts.author: LanieM (Lanie Muir)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you, might have to consider it however would like to see Monday develop this feature as we are pretty tight with 3rd party applications.

posts.author: Juliia (Juliia)
posts.cookedHtml:
@LanieM totally understand the frustration, exporting a specific board item or a form submission as a clean PDF really shouldn’t require an Excel detour.

A couple of options worth knowing about:

If you’re open to third-party apps, there are a few in the marketplace that handle this. Some use Word templates, and others let you work with existing PDF templates — useful if your organisation already has branded forms or documents you want to fill with board data.

That second approach is what PDF Template Filler does — you upload your own PDF, drag placeholders onto the fields, and it auto-fills them from your board items. Could save you the reformatting time you mentioned. Here’s a quick demo if you want to see how it works.

That said, totally respect the hesitation around third-party apps, if that ever changes, it might be worth revisiting.
